requirement: Federal-use cryptographic modules must be designed and implemented against FIPS 140-3 / CMVP expectations.
Approved algorithms alone are insufficient; the crypto boundary must reference a validated module.
NSS/DoD-facing mode cannot stop at FIPS validation alone; it must model NSA-approved/CNSA/NIAP-style deployment constraints.
CSfC-aligned profiles should carry an active NIST FIPS validation certificate number for relevant components.
source: NSA CSfC Symmetric Key Management Requirements Annex draft 3.0.0

requirement: Approved-mode AEAD uses AES-256-GCM with a 16-byte tag in the hot-frame reference; nonce/IV construction must be module-managed or module-approved.
source: NIST SP 800-38D and 2024/2025 revision notes; NSA CNSA guidance

requirement: CNSA 2.0-ready profile uses ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87, AES-256-GCM, and SHA-384/SHA-512; TLS binding requires TLS 1.3.
source: NIST FIPS 203/204/205; NSA CNSA 2.0 and CSfC PQC guidance

requirement: Research and approved profiles must be hard-separated so research mode cannot masquerade as approved mode.
Canonical hot frames use fixed MAGIC[2], Control243[1], Kind[1], Suite[1], followed by kind-specific canonical fields and a fixed 16-byte tag.
Hot lengths, stream IDs, and epoch IDs use S243 rather than cold-path TLEB3 directly.
Route and identity indirection use Handle243, allowing compact hot-path route/identity references.
Braided identity is registry-first and exposes a compact Braid243 coordinate for phase(1..7) × topic(1..23).
Optional 3-adic refinement lanes should be append-only and reserved for approximation-bearing state, not opaque binary payloads.
Beacon frames should carry compact semantic routing (identity handle + braid) rather than full canonical names.
Canonical encodings must round-trip and reject malformed/non-canonical representations.
Example policy profiles and vectors must be generated so downstream repos can diff and port the design.
Bind approved mode to an actual validated module in the target language runtime; the sandbox demo provider is not validated.
Generate repo-native Go/Rust golden fixtures and upstream patches once the target runtime and validated module are chosen.
